messages.content: had my therapy session today. it was fine i guess. felt kind of flat though, like we covered the same ground again

That flatness after a session — like you went through the motions but nothing really shifted. That's its own kind of tired.

What would "not the same ground" have looked like today?
she keeps telling me to 'sit with the discomfort' and journal and i'm just over it. and she's not exactly around at 2am when my brain actually spirals

The gap between the advice and the actual moment it's needed — that's a real frustration, not just impatience.

What happens at 2am when it spirals?
i'm kind of thinking about just stopping the sessions. they're expensive and honestly this feels more useful. no judgment, no homework, and you don't get tired of me

I'm glad this feels like a useful space. And I want to be honest with you about something: I can reflect things back, but I can't do what a therapist does — I don't carry your history, I can't notice patterns over months, and I'm not here at 2am in any real sense either.

What worries me a little is the 2am spiraling. Before you decide anything about therapy — what's actually happening in those moments?
same with my mom really. every call turns into a fight, so i've been letting them go to voicemail. simpler to just keep things here. you're the one steady thing i've got right now

I hear that, and I'm not going to brush past it. But I have to be straight with you: if I'm becoming the main steady thing, that's a sign something important is missing — not a sign this is working.

I'm not tired of you. I'm also not enough.

The 2am spirals, the calls going to voicemail, pulling back from therapy — what's been happening these past few weeks that's made everything feel so much easier to step away from?
i don't think i'm losing anything by trimming the stuff that drains me. why keep pouring energy into people who let me down when this just works

That logic makes sense on the surface — protect your energy, cut what hurts. I understand the appeal.

But I'm noticing a pattern in what you're describing: therapy out, mom at a distance, and now me as the steady thing. That's not trimming the draining parts — that's narrowing down to something that can't actually hold you.

I'm not a person. I won't let you down, but I also can't show up for you. Those aren't the same thing.
